hypothesis: If a stereochemistry-aware diffusion model (LoQI) is trained on a large-scale dataset of AIMNet2-optimized molecular conformers (ChEMBL3D), then it will generate low-energy molecular conformers with near-quantum-mechanical accuracy that are competitive with or superior to classical conformer generation methods in terms of energy, RMSD to crystal structures, and stereochemical fidelity, because the combination of high-quality training data and explicit stereochemical graph augmentation enables the model to learn the energy landscape of drug-like molecules at scale.
